reforms.description: The Australian government increased funding to encourage quality research and upgrade university research infrastructure, which is crucial for enhancing the innovation system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing research infrastructure supports innovation capacity and productivity growth in the long term.
reforms.status_evidence: Increased government funding was allocated for research and infrastructure upgrades.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in research infrastructure, which is essential for fostering innovation and improving research quality.
reforms.source_quote: Other key initiatives include increased government funding to encourage quality research and upgrade university research infrastructure.

reforms.description: Australia's innovation system is supported by funding for additional targeted university places, prioritizing programs in ICT, mathematics, and science to enhance foundational skills necessary for successful innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of skilled graduates in critical fields, the reform aims to enhance the innovation capacity of the workforce, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Funding is to be provided for additional targeted university places each year.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in human capital aimed at fostering innovation, particularly in key technological and scientific fields.
reforms.source_quote: Funding is also to be provided for 2000 additional targeted university places each year with priority on programmes in the areas of ICT, mathematics and science.

reforms.description: The Backing Australia’s Ability programme, launched in January 2001, aims to provide a comprehensive and integrated package to boost infrastructure and research funding, addressing barriers to innovation in Australia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public investment in R&D, which can enhance innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was launched to address weaknesses in R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme represents a significant commitment to enhancing Australia's R&D infrastructure and funding, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Backing Australia’s Ability programme, launched in January 2001, is designed to provide a comprehensive and integrated package boosting infrastructure and research funding.

reforms.description: The Australian government launched a A$3 billion innovation package, Backing Australia’s Ability, aimed at enhancing the country's capacity for research, encouraging the commercialization of ideas, and developing skills over the next five years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The package is designed to stimulate R&D investment and innovation, which are critical for economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures are set to be implemented over the next five years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in innovation infrastructure and R&D, aiming to close the gap with OECD averages and enhance Australia's competitive position.
reforms.source_quote: Recognising the importance of innovation as a driver of sustainable economic growth and increased competitiveness, the government launched in January 2001 a A$3 billion innovation package, Backing Australia’s Ability.

reforms.description: The R&D tax concession scheme will be reformed to enhance the 125% tax concession and introduce a premium rate of 175% for additional labor-related R&D expenditure, along with a tax rebate for small firms making a loss.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing business R&D spending, the reform aims to stimulate innovation and enhance productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is part of the broader innovation package set to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the existing tax concession framework, aiming to boost business investment in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The new arrangements involve a reform of the R&D tax concession scheme, which is the principal mechanism to increase business spending on R&D in Australia.

reforms.description: The R&D Start program will be extended to provide competitive grants for R&D projects, aiming to support innovation and research activities in various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted funding for R&D projects, the program aims to stimulate innovation and enhance collaboration between public and private sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The program is part of the broader innovation package set to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The extension of the R&D Start program represents a significant enhancement of funding opportunities for innovative projects.
reforms.source_quote: The R&D Start program, which is primarily focused on providing grants on a competitive basis for R&D projects, will also be extended.

reforms.description: The National Action Plan for Salinity and Water Quality represents Australia's first targeted national strategy to address dryland salinity, supported by A$1.4 billion in public financing over seven years.
reforms.package_name: National Action Plan for Salinity and Water Quality
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to improve land and water management, which can enhance agricultural productivity and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Action Plan for Salinity and Water Quality is described as a recently-released strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant shift in national policy towards a coordinated approach to a critical environmental issue, with substantial funding and long-term goals.
reforms.source_quote: The recently-released National Action Plan for Salinity and Water Quality is the first concerted and targeted national strategy to address the salinity problem, in particular dryland salinity, supported by a A$1.4 billion public financing.

reforms.description: The Greenhouse Gas Abatement Programme (GGAP) was established to provide A$400 million over four years for competitive subsidies aimed at greenhouse gas abatement projects, including renewable energy and energy efficiency initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions through innovative projects, thereby supporting environmental sustainability and potentially enhancing economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was operational with funding allocated for greenhouse gas abatement projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: The GGAP represents a significant investment in environmental innovation, targeting multiple projects to reduce emissions, but it is not a comprehensive overhaul of existing policies.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the Measures for a Better Environment, announced at the end of 1999... A$400 million of public money is to be provided over the next four years for large-scale greenhouse gas emission reductions.

reforms.description: A$264 million was allocated for the conversion of diesel-fuelled electricity generators to renewable energy technology in remote areas, aiming to enhance the use of renewable energy sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative promotes the transition to renewable energy, which can lead to long-term sustainability and reduced reliance on fossil fuels.
reforms.status_evidence: The allocation of funds for the conversion of generators was part of the broader environmental measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a targeted effort to enhance renewable energy use in specific regions, contributing to environmental goals but not fundamentally altering the energy policy landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Another measure included in that same package is the allocation of up to A$264 million over the next four years to the conversion of diesel-fuelled electricity generators to renewable energy technology in remote, off-grid areas.

reforms.description: The Government released 'Backing Australia’s Ability–An Innovation Action Plan for Australia', which outlines a strategy to promote education, research, and its commercialisation, involving an investment of A$3.0 billion over five years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment aims to enhance research capabilities and commercialisation efforts, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was released and outlines specific funding initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in the innovation ecosystem, aiming to enhance research funding and infrastructure, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The plan outlines the next steps in the Government’s strategy to promote education, and research and its commercialisation.

reforms.description: The plan includes a doubling of funding for Australian Research Council grants and an additional A$914 million for research infrastructure in universities and major national research facilities.
reforms.component_name: Research Council Grants and Infrastructure Funding
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing funding for research grants and infrastructure is expected to enhance research output and innovation capacity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding initiatives were part of the released plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the funding landscape for research, which is critical for fostering innovation and maintaining competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The major initiatives include a doubling of funding for Australian Research Council grants and an additional A$914 million for research infrastructure.

reforms.description: The plan includes an extension of the Research and Development (R&D) Start Programme, aimed at small and medium-sized enterprises, along with reforms to the R&D tax concession.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting SMEs through R&D initiatives is likely to enhance their innovation capabilities, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The extension of the programme was included in the released plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant for SMEs, enhancing their access to R&D support, but it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The plan includes an extension of the Research and Development (R&D) Start Programme, targeted at small and medium-sized enterprises.
